title: Reflected XSS / Attribute Injection in Search
status_code: 5000: 200 OK with unsafe reflection, 5001: 200 OK with escaped output
vulnerable_behavior: The vulnerable app reflects the search query using unsafe rendering. Depending on context, this can appear as text-node XSS or attribute injection in the input value attribute.
secure_behavior: The secure app lets Jinja escape the value by default, preventing the input from being interpreted as HTML or attributes.
evidence_note: Inspect the rendered HTML response and compare how the query is reflected in both apps.
root_cause: The vulnerable template marks user-controlled search input as safe before rendering it into HTML.
impact: Unsafe reflection may allow attacker-controlled HTML or script execution in a user's browser in a real deployment.
remediation: Do not mark user input as safe. Keep autoescaping enabled and apply context-aware output encoding.
retest_notes: Retest confirmed that the secure app escapes the same input instead of rendering it as HTML.
